texto: We can never stop searching for Heaven, since there is always more of it than we can see. There, as in those tales that evolve endlessly into other tales, stories have no end. They are hardly ever the stories you know, the official ones, in which wishes are made formal, then legislated and enforced as matters of life or death. They are more often the stories we didn’t hear, or wouldn’t believe, told by the person we ignored, the house that was razed, the choir of dry bones. The scholars of Heaven read and study the vast collection of ashes, books from the torched libraries.
explicacao: Citação de Patricia Storace sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “We can never stop searching for Heaven, since there is always more of it than we can see. There, …” articula educacao, mythology, religion em registro de educacao, com relevância para contextos como luto e estudos.

texto: And what does God ask of you?” “To fly without wings. To do what seems impossible. To cling to our loves through the rapids of our hates. To know that God charges us with the task of making these miracles. They exist between person and person, as flame goes from candle to candle. To know that all we can know of right or wrong is through the way our acts are revealed in the lives of other people. There is no other way to see God. It is not given to us.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Patricia Storace que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “And what does God ask of you?” “To fly without wings. To do what seems impossible. To cling to ou…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre fe, god, morality, com foco em morality e religion.

texto: They lay together like a word and a comma, asleep within an unfinished sentence.

texto: Many more have died of attempting love than victory, and countless numbers hate love more than war. Honor has often been the dear prize awarded to the killers of lovers. The epics of war have always and still outnumber the epics of love. For those who love deeply and greatly gain a clairvoyant, excruciating awareness of the fear and suffering of the world along with their joy, which few warriors could endure. Who is not more truly afraid of a love story than of a tale of war?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Patricia Storace que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “Many more have died of attempting love than victory, and countless numbers hate love more than wa…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, love, mythology, com foco em termino e mythology.

texto: You cannot know how many other miracles one sole miracle begets. She was not a savior. She did not confer salvation. She said we were to be the saviors. She said we know God only through each other. That God is not in us, but between us. That we deliver God to each other, like a flame passed from cupped hand to cupped hand. We bring God to life in each other.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Patricia Storace: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. texto: In a story, you must always listen for the voice you cannot hear, the one that has been ignored or silenced. In that crushed voice, there is a strain of truth, as a crushed grape yields a drop of wine.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Patricia Storace sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “In a story, you must always listen for the voice you cannot hear, the one that has been ignored o…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, feminism, storytelling, útil em momentos de reflexao-noturna e dia-chuvoso.

texto: Souraya thought those days that she knew so many songs about the misery of love because pain kept love within the boundaries of time, and knowable, whereas what she was experiencing passed beyond the horizon of birth and death; it was like the eternal life the prophets spoke of. A gesture, a kiss, a task, a sentence had a golden elemental endlessness, like the scenes in the murals painted in the island houses.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Patricia Storace: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo.
